learning_outcomes.description: identify learning difficulties and reflect on the features of the difficulties in particular content areas that should be assessed;
plan instruction to address students needs;
implement effective pedagogical practices for students with learning difficulties in particular content areas;
implement methods to monitor students learning and progress;
complete literature searches using a variety of databases to demonstrate wide reading and deep thinking;
access, critique and synthesise theory and/or research and use contemporary literature to develop and sustain argument(s); and,
demonstrate written communication skills (consistent with appropriate conventions of the American Psychological Association Publication Manual).

assessments.task_description:
This assessment consists of two components: a 15-minute oral presentation and a 5–7 minute question-and-answer (Q&A) session.

Oral Presentation:

For this task, you are required to prepare and deliver a 15-minute presentation on formative assessment as a tool for inclusion in assessment practice. Formative assessment is typically conducted before or during the teaching process and enables teachers to make informed decisions about instructional content and teaching strategies. The purpose of this presentation is to demonstrate your ability to conduct research, communicate key ideas, and show your understanding of how formative assessment tools and techniques can be applied in practice.

You will be provided with a specific student context by your lecturer/tutor. Using this context, you are required to design a hypothetical formative assessment plan for a school-aged student experiencing difficulties with reading.

Your presentation should include:

  • A clear definition of formative assessment
  • An explanation of how formative assessment informs teaching and learning
  • A description of at least two formative assessment tools or techniques
  • A practical explanation of how these tools would be used to assess the student in relation to the Australian Curriculum achievement standards
  • A critical discussion, supported by research literature, of the advantages and disadvantages of the selected tools
  • A persuasive argument explaining why these strategies are effective for supporting students with reading difficulties and promoting inclusive education

Presentations must be delivered live—either in class for on-campus students or online for external students. Pre-recorded presentations will not be accepted. You are required to use PowerPoint slides to support your presentation.

Q&A session

Following your presentation, you will participate in a 5–7 minute Q&A session with your peers and lecturer/tutor.

You are expected to participate actively in this session as both: a questioner, and an answerer. Each student is expected to:

  • Ask at least one constructive and relevant question after a peer’s presentation
  • Respond to questions asked about their own presentation

The Q&A session must occur spontaneously and naturally. Pre-prepared or negotiated questions are not permitted, and any evidence of this will result in the question and response being deemed invalid. You are not allowed to use notes, scripts, or any other resources during the Q&A session.

Assessment 1 will commence in Week 4. Please note that all presentations will be recorded for assessment purposes. The specific student context on which your presentation will be based will be provided in Week 2, giving you time to prepare prior to the assessment.

AI usage guidelines for Assessment task 1:

If you use AI and/or machine translation (MT) technologies in any way in preparing the ppt slides and notes for your presentation, you must provide specific details about how it was used. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute academic misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Guidelines about conventions for appropriate referencing and acknowledgement, including an example coversheet to provide details of use, are available here: Overview - AI tools for assignments - Library Guides at University of Queensland Library

The Q&A session is specifically designed to ensure academic integrity and assess your understanding of course concepts. You are expected to:

  • Ask questions independently and spontaneously
  • Provide immediate and informed responses

The use of AI, notes, or any external resources during the Q&A session is strictly prohibited.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You will submit your slides at least a day ahead of your allocated presentation day
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: As this is a pre-scheduled presentation, late submission is not permitted without and approved extension request via myUQ. Any late presentation deemed late will receive a grade of 0 for the task and a failing grade for the course.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.assessment_title: Assessment 2: Essay on Planning and Instruction
assessments.weighting: <dd>30% 1800 words (excl. References) for essay and 12 minute oral presentation </dd>
assessments.task_description:
Teacher’s work involves decision making about planning and instruction. In this assessment task you will be asked to focus on the relationship between planning and instruction for student/s who require support within Quality Differentiated Teaching Practices (QDTP). This task requires you to outline the steps teachers need to make in planning their teaching (instruction). Make reference to the curriculum (what the teacher teaches) and the pedagogy (how the teacher teaches) for students requiring QDTP in your preferred year level.

The decision making will be influenced by (a) department/system policies, (b) the teacher’s beliefs about teaching and learning, (c) the classroom setting, and (d) the students’ abilities, (e) their previous learning experiences and prior knowledge, and (f) their interests. Provide an analysis of how these variables impact on the curriculum and pedagogy used to teach these students. Provide evidence for your arguments by making reference to the literature.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Assignments for this course will be submitted electronically via Blackboard and using Turnitin.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 1 grade for each 24 hour period from time submission is due will apply for up to 7 days. After 7 days you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
30/10/2026 2:00 pm

Due date and time is Australian Eastern Standard Time (AEST)
assessments.task_description:
Case Study of a student with learning difficulty

The case study requires you to demonstrate that you can identify the critical characteristics of the child’s learning and have an idea of how to address their needs.

The following prompts can be used to write the case study.

  • Based on the description of the student suggest how you think the difficulty might impact in success in the nominated subject.
  • Generate what assessment measures or techniques you would use to explore the student’s achievement. In other words, indicate the observations, tests or assessments you would use to check your initial understanding of his difficulties. Your assessment should guide your decision-making in developing an intervention for the child rather than be aimed at categorising or classifying the child as having some form of difficulty. It is not necessary to use a formal assessment measure such as a standardised test. However, when using these assessment measures or techniques you should be able to collect information in a systematic way that will tell you precisely why the student is failing to learn.
  • Assuming that you are correct in your analysis, develop an intervention plan to address the student’s difficulties. The plan should include the content or skills you would teach as well as the teaching strategies you would use. You do not have to give a detailed programme, just a general overview of the skills and understandings which the student needs to develop.
  • Describe how you would monitor the student’s progress.
